commit.message:
feat(skills): 新增 codex-session-reader skill (#30)

## Why
- 需要一个仅面向 Codex 的只读 session/thread 阅读 skill，便于在已知 thread id 时直接查看会话内容。
- 通过 `codex app-server` 官方接口读取数据，可以避免耦合底层本地存储格式，并降低后续维护成本。

## What
- 新增 `codex-session-reader` skill，提供 `read` 子命令，通过 `codex app-server`
读取单个 Codex thread。
- 支持 `--preview-only` 与 0-based、接近 Python 切片语法的 `--turns` 参数，用于控制输出范围。
- 更新根目录 `README.md` 的 skills 表格，补充 `codex-session-reader` 与遗漏的
`coderabbit-cli` 条目。

## Testing
- `uvx ruff check
skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py --help`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
--help`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
<thread-id> --preview-only`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
<thread-id> --turns :5`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
<thread-id> --turns -5:`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
<thread-id> --turns 10:-1`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
<thread-id> --turns 13`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
<thread-id> --turns 1:10:2`（确认报错）

---------

commit.message:
docs(examples): refresh onboarding and prune stale scripts

Summary:
- rewrite README around first-time EtherCAT bring-up and current
  public APIs
- fix docs and examples indices to point users at the maintained
  Livebooks first
- migrate the remaining useful low-level example scripts to the
  current Bus and EtherCAT APIs
- remove obsolete example scripts built around deleted Master and
  Bus APIs

Rationale:
- the repo still exposed broken example entrypoints that were worse
  than having fewer examples at all
- first-time users need an actionable path from bus discovery to
  process-data exchange without prior EtherCAT knowledge
- keeping only current examples lowers support load and makes the
  repo's harness surface more trustworthy

Validation:
- elixir -e 'for f <- ["examples/scan.exs", "examples/bench.exs",
  "examples/udp_test.exs", "examples/loopback.exs",
  "examples/sdo_debug.exs"] do
    Code.string_to_quoted!(File.read!(f), file: f)
  end'
- mix ethercat.harness.doctor
- mix test

commit.message:
refactor(harness): add docs spine and repo doctor

Summary:
- add docs entrypoints, design and execution plan indices, a quality
  scorecard, and a harness-engineering design doc
- slim AGENTS.md into a routing layer and narrow usage_rules syncing to
  Elixir and OTP guidance only
- add `mix ethercat.harness.doctor` to mechanically validate the docs
  spine and AGENTS authored section size
- update README contributor guidance and add focused doctor tests

Rationale:
- the repo already had strong subsystem docs, but it lacked the
  structure and mechanical checks needed for an agent-first workflow
- AGENTS.md should act as a table of contents, while `docs/` remains the
  system of record for design history, plans, and quality tracking
- the doctor keeps this structure from drifting without turning the repo
  into documentation theater

Validation:
- mix ethercat.harness.doctor
- mix test test/ethercat/harness/doctor_test.exs
- mix test

commit.message:
fix(api): enforce signal direction and clarify staging

Summary:
- reject writing registered input signals and reading registered output
  signals in EtherCAT.Slave
- verify staged output bytes after `write_output/3` and document that the
  API confirms domain-buffer staging, not physical pin application
- rename master state fields to `slave_configs` and `domain_configs`
  and remove the unused slave-station Registry entry
- update slave/master architecture docs and add focused slave tests

Rationale:
- the public PDO API should enforce input/output direction explicitly
  instead of letting callers misuse registered signals
- generic PDO writes cannot honestly guarantee that hardware has already
  applied a value, so the API contract now matches what the runtime can
  really prove
- the master field rename and registry cleanup reduce ambiguity and keep
  internal naming aligned with the actual data shape

Validation:
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(dc): align distributed clocks runtime and API

Summary:
- add a spec-focused DC review, public API proposal, and execution plan
- replace the old DC surface with `EtherCAT.DC.Config`, `DC.Status`,
  `dc_status/0`, `reference_clock/0`, and `await_dc_locked/1`
- keep DC as a separate runtime process with its own FRMW-based
  maintenance and lock diagnostics instead of coupling it to domains
- move slave sync config and CoE helpers under `EtherCAT.Slave.Sync.*`
  and expose named sync/latch intent through slave config
- add a hardware validation Livebook and update examples, docs, and
  tests for the new runtime shape

Rationale:
- DC is a network-wide clock subsystem, not a domain concern, so the
  runtime owner should stay separate from process-image domains
- the public API now distinguishes clearly between master-wide DC
  infrastructure and per-slave sync behavior
- the new docs and notebook make the current behavior easier to verify
  on real hardware and easier to compare against the EtherCAT spec

Validation:
- mix test
- parsed all Elixir blocks in
  examples/hardware_validation_livebook.livemd

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Improve local URL handling and E2E stability

Make marketplace terms public, centralize app URL usage for redirects and metadata, add AGENT.md symlink support, and harden flaky Playwright flows for more reliable local runs.

commit.message:
Clarify post-fix local-time GEFS cadence in AGENTS

Document the timezone pitfall explicitly: Slurm SubmitTime is local wall time (MST/MDT), not UTC.

Record expected post-fix mapping for local submit slots:
- 03:15 -> 00Z
- 09:15 -> 06Z
- 15:15 -> 12Z
- 21:15 -> 18Z

Also note that pre-fix historical logs may show incorrect init mapping.

commit.message:
Fix Slurm submit-time timezone handling for auto-init

Root cause: Slurm SubmitTime was parsed as UTC even though it is local wall time, causing 03:15 local jobs to resolve to 18Z instead of 00Z.

Fix: convert SubmitTime to epoch using local-time interpretation, then compute init anchor in UTC from epoch before 4.5h offset + 6-hour rounding.

Validation:
- bash -n scripts/submit_clyfar.sh
- sanity check: 2026-03-06T03:15:05 local => init 2026030600
- full rerun: job 11264523 (init 2026030600) completed with JSON/PNG/LLM uploads

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: John Lawson <john.rob.lawson@googlemail.com>

Fix LaTeX warning from chapter reference (#317)

Use <Ref Chap='Chapter_Tutorials'/> in Magic.gd instead of
<Ref Label='Chapter_Tutorials'/> to avoid invalid LaTeX
reference expansion with escaped underscores.

commit.message:
chore: roll out gh-aw review workflows (#42)

## Summary
- Roll out GitHub Agentic Workflows initialization and shared PR review
workflows.
- Pin `gh-aw` in `mise.toml` and add a dedicated validation workflow for
agentic workflow changes.

## Commands Executed
```text
git checkout -b codex/gh-aw-rollout
gh aw version
gh aw init
gh aw add pulumi-labs/gh-aw-internal/.github/workflows/gh-aw-pr-review.md@main --name tool-cdk2pulumi-pr-review --force
gh aw add pulumi-labs/gh-aw-internal/.github/workflows/gh-aw-pr-rereview.md@main --name tool-cdk2pulumi-pr-rereview --force
gh aw compile
gh aw validate
git status --short
git add .gitattributes mise.toml .github/agents/agentic-workflows.agent.md .github/aw/actions-lock.json .github/aw/imports/.gitattributes .github/aw/imports/pulumi-labs/gh-aw-internal/99a309bf18258dbae98c8176ea10657e0b08deca/.github_snippets_code-review.md .github/workflows/copilot-setup-steps.yml .github/workflows/tool-cdk2pulumi-pr-rereview.md .github/workflows/tool-cdk2pulumi-pr-rereview.lock.yml .github/workflows/tool-cdk2pulumi-pr-review.md .github/workflows/tool-cdk2pulumi-pr-review.lock.yml .github/workflows/tool-cdk2pulumi-validate-agentic-workflows.yml
git commit -m 'chore: roll out gh-aw review workflows'
git push -u origin codex/gh-aw-rollout
```

## Resolved Values
- `base_branch`: `main`
- `prefix_stem`: `tool-cdk2pulumi`
- `gh aw init`: ran

## Changed Files
- `.gitattributes`
- `.github/agents/agentic-workflows.agent.md`
- `.github/aw/actions-lock.json`
- `.github/aw/imports/.gitattributes`
-
`.github/aw/imports/pulumi-labs/gh-aw-internal/99a309bf18258dbae98c8176ea10657e0b08deca/.github_snippets_code-review.md`
- `.github/workflows/copilot-setup-steps.yml`
- `.github/workflows/tool-cdk2pulumi-pr-rereview.lock.yml`
- `.github/workflows/tool-cdk2pulumi-pr-rereview.md`
- `.github/workflows/tool-cdk2pulumi-pr-review.lock.yml`
- `.github/workflows/tool-cdk2pulumi-pr-review.md`
- `.github/workflows/tool-cdk2pulumi-validate-agentic-workflows.yml`
- `mise.toml`

## Validation
### `gh aw compile`
```text

Ensure proper audience validation and trust policies are configured.

OIDC tokens can authenticate to cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP).
Ensure proper audience validation and trust policies are configured.

✓ .github/workflows/tool-cdk2pulumi-pr-review.md (67.4 KB)

```

commit.message:
refactor(runtime): harden startup and slave lifecycle

Summary:
- extract pure master startup helpers for config normalization, INIT reset,
  INIT recovery, and INIT verification
- retain last terminal master failure and relax broadcast INIT ack handling
  to tolerate partial positive WKC before per-station verification
- tighten slave AL transitions, checked PREOP and SAFEOP post-transition
  configuration, and subscriber/input dispatch internals
- update public docs for the explicit PREOP/SAFEOP flow and sm-keyed
  signal decoding

Rationale:
- the startup sequence now reads closer to the EtherCAT state-machine and
  reset flow instead of mixing policy with runtime orchestration
- hardware showed that broadcast reset writes are not a reliable all-slave
  barrier, so per-station INIT verification must remain the real gate
- slave configuration should fail on checked mailbox, AL, and DC write
  errors instead of silently accepting partial state

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/master/init_reset_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

increase directory preview limit of MediaInput

ensure UI responsive in the ctx of deep recursive scans through a 250-item cap

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
chore(symphony): add crowd-snake workflow assets

Summary:
- add a Symphony workflow under `.symphony/WORKFLOW.md` for crowd-snake
- import the relevant repo-local skills under `.codex/skills`
- document the workflow and Human Review semantics in AGENTS and README

Rationale:
- keep the project compatible with the platform workflow path contract
- let managed agents review PRs in Human Review and auto-return issues to Rework
- make the workflow, skills, and repository runtime contract self-consistent

Tests:
- `go run ./cmd/workflow-validate /Users/s.retivykh/projects/symphony-orchestra/crowd-snake/.symphony/WORKFLOW.md`
- `git -C /Users/s.retivykh/projects/symphony-orchestra/crowd-snake diff --check -- .symphony/WORKFLOW.md AGENTS.md README.md`
- not run: `./scripts/smoke-test.sh` (workflow/docs/skills-only change)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

keep media-bar visible upon bulk processing completion

Users need static visual confirmation when a bulk processing run finishes.
Leaving the primary progress bar on the screen clarifies that the entire
operation has concluded, without the clutter of secondary and transient tracking
bars lingering alongside it. Strict event ordering is needed during finalization
The frontend must receive the absolute final progress updates before the cleanup
signal arrives, avoiding out-of-order execution artifacts on the UI.

∙ Emit a completion lifecycle event to selectively prune frontend progress bars
∙ Synchronize progress batch flush emission to guarantee logical arrival order

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-Authored-By: Gemini <noreply@deepmind.google>
